note: Private mutation/regression corpus for SwiftIdempotency — the kit dogfooding on itself (Chapter 30 §30.4.4). Mutants live in the library's own source; each is killed by the library's own tests, including the negative-control `withKnownIssue` tests whose whole job is to prove the detector isn't blind. Run via `swift test --filter`, which targets methods precisely under SPM. Not a scored benchmark — no frozen answer key.

mutants.label: assertIdempotentProperty runs the operation once (second = first) instead of twice — a non-retry-idempotent op is no longer detected
assertIdempotentEffectsProperty drops the second (retry) pass — afterSecond == afterFirst always, so added effects on replay go unseen
IdempotencyKey(fromEntity:) derives from the entity, not its .id — the rawValue is no longer the stable identifier
